herb_constituents.name: quercetin

herb_constituents.name: kaempferol
herb_constituents.desc: Astragalus membranaceus kaempferol, a flavonoid compound, has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Research has shown that kaempferol can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thus reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Additionally, kaempferol has been found to have protective effects against cardiovascular disease, neurodegenerative disorders, and certain types of cancer.

herb_constituents.name: triterpenoids
herb_constituents.desc: Astragalus membranaceus triterpenoids are a group of bioactive compounds extracted from the root of the Astragalus plant, which has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. herb_constituents.name: coumarins
herb_constituents.desc: Astragalus membranaceus coumarins, which are a type of lignan, have been found to possess anticoagulant properties and may help prevent blood clots from forming. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects of coumarins in Astragalus membranaceus and their potential medicinal applications.
